last_excerpt: words are plucked, gentle, hesitant—others are hammered out, forceful, insistent, the syllables landing like blows. The rhythm of command, announcement, interruption. In a room somewhere, an upright piano leans against a wall, hammers poised above their assigned wires, silent unless summoned, every note asleep until woken by a key. The wild unpredictability of the hammered note: sometimes too loud, sometimes ghostly, a missed shot, a double strike. Unforgiving, but alive. There is a mark left on the felt after a thousand strikes: grooves, signs of practice, invisible unless you look inside. The music accumulates a physical record, invisible to an audience but permanent, shaped by every performance. A child discovers the action for the first time, jabbing at keys, delighted by the chaos, not yet seeking melody. Sometimes two notes ring together, one struck, the other caught in its resonance, a string vibrating in sympathy, a harmony no one ordered.
last_concepts: peeling an orange in one piece
a thing you've memorized but never understood
